$comment: ADD secret patterns — executable catalog. Mirror of core/knowledge/secret-patterns.md § 1. Drift between this and the markdown reference is enforced by scripts/validate-secret-patterns.py. JSON shape is intentionally line-oriented and shallow so an awk extractor can read it without jq (see core/lib/scan-secrets.sh).

patterns.name: AWS_ACCESS_KEY
patterns.remediation: Rotate the key in the AWS IAM console immediately. A matching secret-access-key pair is probably in the same file.

patterns.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
patterns.remediation: Revoke at https://github.com/settings/tokens. Regenerate if needed.

patterns.name: STRIPE_LIVE_SECRET
patterns.remediation: Roll the key in the Stripe dashboard. Audit webhook logs for misuse during the exposure window.

patterns.name: JWT
patterns.remediation: Invalidate the session or rotate the signing key. JWTs are bearer tokens — assume compromise once leaked.

patterns.name: PASSWORD_KV
patterns.remediation: Rotate the password; move to a secret manager or .env (which .secretsignore covers by default).

patterns.name: PEM_PRIVATE_KEY
patterns.remediation: ROTATE IMMEDIATELY. Private keys are the highest-severity leak. Delete the file from git history (git filter-repo), notify anyone with access to the repo, generate a new key.
